fact: November 18 is exactly 322 days into the year, which means there are only 43 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when squirrels go absolutely nuts burying their winter stash - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns and remember where most of them are!
The sun sets before 5 PM in most northern cities around this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're still doing homework!
November 18 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season, when people born under this zodiac sign are supposed to be extra mysterious and intense.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, November 18 is when jacaranda trees explode with bright purple flowers that carpet the ground like nature's confetti!
This date sits exactly 4 weeks before the winter solstice in the north, meaning daylight is shrinking by about 2-3 minutes every single day!
Polar bears in the Arctic are just starting to hunt on newly frozen sea ice around this time, after fasting for months during the ice-free summer!
November 18 is when maple trees in cold regions have dropped most of their leaves, but oak trees stubbornly hang onto their brown, crunchy leaves until spring!
The moon phase on November 18 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it rises right around sunset and stays visible all night long!
In northern places, the first hard frost that kills most garden plants typically happens around mid-November, turning green lawns into crunchy, sparkly carpets by morning!
November 18 is when many birds that migrate have already reached their winter homes, but Arctic terns are still flying - they migrate 44,000 miles per year!
The air gets so dry in many places by this date that you can shock people just by touching them after walking across carpet!
